title: v3: use autoloops SDK as orchestration backend
description:
Epic for the ralph v3 major release. Replace ralph's in-house orchestration engine with the autoloops SDK (@mikeyobrien/autoloop, https://github.com/mikeyobrien/autoloop) as the execution backend.

## Background
- Current version: 2.10.1. v3 is the next major.
- Today ralph only *consumes* autoloop TOML presets (autoloops.toml + topology.toml + roles/*.md), detected in crates/ralph-cli/src/main.rs and resolved via the preset search path (incl. $HOME/.config/autoloop/presets). The orchestration loop itself is ralph's own engine (crates/ralph-core/src/event_loop, hatless_ralph.rs).
- v3 inverts this: autoloops SDK becomes the backend that actually drives loops/topology, with ralph as the thin coordination layer on top.

## Goals
- Adopt autoloops SDK as the runtime backend for orchestration (loop execution, topology, role dispatch).
- Preserve ralph's surfaces where they add value: CLI (run/plan/task/loops/web), memories + tasks, merge queue, parallel worktree loops, RObot/Telegram HITL, waves, TUI, web dashboard.
- Map ralph concepts (hats/events/backpressure/waves) onto autoloops SDK primitives; document the mapping.
- Migration path from 2.x configs/presets; decide what breaks (backwards-compat is not a constraint per project policy).

## Open questions (to resolve before scoping child tasks)
- Is the autoloops SDK consumed as a Rust crate, an external process, or via FFI/bindings?
- Which ralph-core subsystems get deleted vs. wrapped vs. kept (event_loop, wave_tracker, merge_queue, memory/task stores)?
- What is the v3 config schema and migration story from ralph.yml / TOML presets?

## Acceptance (high level)
- ralph run drives a multi-role loop through the autoloops SDK end-to-end (mock + live).
- E2E + smoke suites pass against the new backend.
- Migration guide + CHANGELOG entry for the v3 cutover.

This is an epic; break into child beads once the SDK integration shape is decided.
